sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשְׁקֹד to be sleepless (Arab. شقِذ) Psa 102:8 to watch, Psa 127:1 Ezr 8:29. Figuratively-(a) followed by עַל to watch over any thing, i.e. to attend to it, to fix one’s attention on any thing, Jer 1:12 31:28 44:27 Dan 9:14 Job 21:32 (but Pro 8:34, שָׁקַר עַל דְּלָתֹות is to be taken in its proper sense, to watch at the threshold, to guard the threshold). Isa 29:20, שֹׁקְדֵי אָוֶן “those who watch for iniquity,” who are diligent not to do what is good, but what is evil.-(b) to lie in wait for (used of a leopard); followed by עַל Jer 5:6.

Pual, part. מְשֻׁקָּד (denom. from שָׁקֵד) as if amygdalatus, i.e. made of the form of almond flowers, Exo 25:33, 34 Exo 25:34.
